steps.title: Focus on estate adjustment
steps.content: The claim is that Chekhov portrays Professor Serebrakoff as unable to adjust to living at the country estate. The best quotation must show the professor himself expressing discomfort with the estate setting, not merely show conflict, impracticality, or someone else's criticism.

steps.title: Eliminate indirect evidence
steps.content: Choice A shows that Serebrakoff calls himself a scholar unfamiliar with practical affairs, but that does not specifically show trouble adjusting to the estate. Choice B shows anxiety about being alone with Vanya, so it concerns a relationship conflict rather than country life.

steps.title: Compare the direct quotations
steps.content: Choice D is Vanya attacking Serebrakoff's writings and calling him a hoax, so it is not the professor's own view of the estate. Choice C directly contrasts the professor's former academic world with being plunged into the cottage and condemned to its company. The answer is C.
